title: QA Generator-Pattern Training: discrete search recovers integer generator from target trace
source_attribution: GLM-5 Team, arXiv:2602.15763 [cs.LG], Feb 2026 — §4.1.2 asynchronous RL policy staleness / double-sided importance sampling / optimizer reset. The failure modes this cert eliminates by construction. There is no float policy to drift; training is discrete identification of an integer generator (b_0, e_0) via exhaustive search over m^2=81 starting tuples on S_9. Grounded in cert [209] (QA Signal Generator Inference — canonical integer generator e_t = ((b_{t+1}-b_t-1)%m)+1), cert [256] (QA Orbit-Resonance Attention — uses the trained generator as operating pattern), cert [257] (QA Integer-State Pipeline — training stays inside two-boundary invariant). Reference prototype qa_lab/qa_orbit_resonance_attention.py:identify_generator. Design doc docs/theory/QA_GLM5_ARCHITECTURE_MAPPING.md §Failure-Mode-3. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-19.
theorem_statement: Training in a QA-native architecture is identification of an integer generator pattern (b_0, e_0) in {1..m}^2 whose T-orbit best matches a target trace under exact tuple-equality count. The training procedure is exhaustive discrete search over m^2 starting tuples; no gradients, no float optimizer state (no Adam m/v buffers, no momentum, no EMA), no importance-sampling corrections, no policy-version staleness, no trust region, no optimizer reset. The trained generator is a deterministic function of the target trace: repeated training invocations on identical targets produce bitwise-identical trained generators.

training_path.note: Exhaustive enumeration of all (b_0, e_0) in {1..9}^2; for each, simulate T-orbit of length len(target) and count exact matches; argmax with lexicographic tiebreak on (b_0, e_0).

optimizer.note: No Adam buffers, no momentum, no EMA, no learning rate. No continuous state at all. Deterministic integer enumeration.

evaluation.note: Integer-valued: number of positions k where state_k (T^k applied to candidate generator) equals target_trace[k]. No soft similarity, no cross-entropy, no distance in a learned embedding.

witnesses.note: Fibonacci cosmos orbit trace starting at (1,1)
Lucas cosmos orbit trace starting at (1,3)
Phibonacci cosmos orbit trace starting at (1,4) — this is the canonical_target_trace source
Tribonacci satellite orbit trace starting at (3,3)

design_claim_context: GLM-5 async RL manages float policy drift via staleness bounds (|w_current - w_0| > delta dropped), double-sided importance sampling with trust region [1-eps, 1+h] and hard masking outside, and optimizer reset after each weight push to rollout engine. All of this apparatus exists to manage drift of a continuous object. Remove the float policy and the apparatus becomes unnecessary. Here the 'policy' is an integer generator pattern; there is no continuum of mostly-current policies, no off-policy bias, no gradient.
